keyStudies.title: Effect of pneumatic compression on DOMS and performance recovery
keyStudies.finding: 30-minute Normatec sessions post-exercise significantly reduced perceived soreness and improved range of motion at 24 and 48 hours.

treatments.mechanism: Sequential pneumatic compression mimics the body's natural muscle pump, propelling venous blood and lymphatic fluid back toward the heart. This accelerates metabolic waste clearance, reduces interstitial fluid accumulation, and enhances oxygen delivery to recovering tissues.
treatments.costEstimate: AED 100-200/session or AED 5,000-10,000 for home device

treatments.name: Shockwave Therapy
treatments.description: Non-invasive acoustic wave therapy that sends high-energy sound waves into injured tissue to stimulate healing, break down calcifications, and promote new blood vessel formation. Used for tendinopathies, plantar fasciitis, erectile dysfunction, and musculoskeletal regeneration.

outcomes.name: Tendon & Ligament Healing
outcomes.description: Highly effective for chronic tendinopathies (tennis elbow, Achilles, rotator cuff). Comparable to surgery in some conditions.

outcomes.name: Plantar Fasciitis Resolution
outcomes.description: FDA-approved for chronic plantar fasciitis. 75-80% of patients experience significant pain reduction.

outcomes.name: Erectile Dysfunction
outcomes.description: Low-intensity ESWT improves blood flow and erectile function. Growing evidence base for vasculogenic ED.

quotes.text: Chronic Achilles tendinopathy for 2 years. 5 shockwave sessions and I was back to running pain-free

protocols.label: Tendinopathy
protocols.notes: 2000-3000 pulses per session. Moderate energy. Results typically felt after 3rd session.

protocols.label: ED Protocol
protocols.notes: Low-intensity focused or radial ESWT. Applied to penile shaft and crura.

keyStudies.title: Extracorporeal shockwave therapy for chronic Achilles tendinopathy
keyStudies.finding: ESWT achieved 60% success rate at 4 months for chronic Achilles tendinopathy, comparable to eccentric loading exercise and significantly better than wait-and-see.

treatments.mechanism: Acoustic waves create controlled microtrauma in target tissue, stimulating neovascularization (new blood vessel growth), stem cell recruitment, and growth factor release. This triggers a regenerative cascade that remodels chronic degenerative tissue.

treatments.name: Whole-Body MRI
treatments.description: A preventive full-body MRI scan that screens for early-stage cancers, aneurysms, organ abnormalities, and structural issues before symptoms appear. Offered by Prenuvo, Fountain Life, and similar longevity clinics. No radiation exposure.

outcomes.name: Early Cancer Detection
outcomes.description: Prenuvo reports finding actionable findings in ~14% of scans, including early-stage cancers, aneurysms, and organ abnormalities. No radiation exposure.

outcomes.name: Peace of Mind / Baseline
outcomes.description: Establishes a comprehensive anatomical baseline for future comparison. Most scans return clean, providing significant psychological benefit.

quotes.text: My Prenuvo scan caught a 2cm kidney cyst and mild disc herniation I had no idea about. Worth every penny for the early detection and peace of mind

protocols.label: Baseline Screening
protocols.notes: 60-90 minute scan. No radiation, no contrast dye needed. Wear comfortable clothing.

protocols.label: Annual Screening
protocols.notes: Annual re-scan allows comparison to baseline. Ideal for high-risk individuals or proactive longevity screening.

keyStudies.title: Incidental findings on whole-body MRI in the general population
keyStudies.finding: Whole-body MRI screening of 2,500+ asymptomatic adults found clinically significant abnormalities in 18% of participants, with 2% having findings requiring immediate treatment.

treatments.mechanism: MRI uses strong magnetic fields and radio waves to create detailed cross-sectional images of every organ, tissue, and structure in the body. Diffusion-weighted imaging (DWI) can detect early tumors based on restricted water molecule movement in dense cellular tissue.

treatments.name: Biological Age Testing
treatments.description: DNA methylation-based tests (TruAge, GrimAge, Horvath Clock, DunedinPACE) that measure biological age vs. chronological age. The gold standard for tracking the effectiveness of longevity interventions. Bryan Johnson famously reduced his biological age by 5+ years.

outcomes.name: Biological Age Measurement
outcomes.description: DNA methylation clocks accurately predict all-cause mortality and disease risk better than chronological age. GrimAge and DunedinPACE are the most validated.

outcomes.name: Intervention Tracking
outcomes.description: Serial testing can measure the impact of lifestyle changes, supplements, and pharmaceuticals on biological aging rate.

quotes.text: TruAge test showed biological age 4 years younger than chronological. Retested after 6 months of protocol changes and gained another year

protocols.label: Baseline + Annual
protocols.notes: TruAge or GrimAge test. Fasting blood draw. Results in 2-4 weeks. Compare year-over-year.

protocols.label: Intervention Tracking
protocols.notes: More frequent testing when actively changing longevity protocols to measure impact.

keyStudies.title: DNA methylation GrimAge strongly predicts lifespan and healthspan
keyStudies.finding: GrimAge epigenetic clock predicts time-to-death, time-to-cancer, and time-to-coronary heart disease more accurately than other clocks and traditional biomarkers.

treatments.mechanism: Epigenetic clocks measure DNA methylation patterns at specific CpG sites across the genome. These methylation patterns change predictably with age and are influenced by lifestyle, diet, and environmental factors. The aggregate pattern produces a 'biological age' score.

treatments.name: Hormone Panel Testing
treatments.description: Comprehensive blood testing of sex hormones, thyroid function, adrenal hormones, and metabolic markers. Foundation of functional medicine and hormone optimization protocols. Essential baseline for anyone considering TRT, HRT, or longevity interventions.

outcomes.name: Baseline Establishment
outcomes.description: Identifies hormonal imbalances, thyroid dysfunction, and metabolic issues before symptoms become severe. Essential for personalized treatment.

outcomes.name: Treatment Monitoring
outcomes.description: Enables precise dose titration for TRT, HRT, thyroid medication, and other hormonal interventions.

quotes.text: My comprehensive panel revealed subclinical hypothyroidism and low testosterone — neither had been caught by standard check-ups. Game-changer for my health optimization

protocols.label: Comprehensive Panel
protocols.notes: Includes: Total/Free T, Estradiol, DHEA-S, Cortisol, TSH, Free T3/T4, FSH, LH, SHBG, Progesterone, Insulin, HbA1c. Morning draw, fasting.

protocols.label: Follow-up Panel
protocols.notes: Re-test to track intervention effectiveness. More frequent if actively titrating hormones.

keyStudies.title: The importance of comprehensive hormone testing in clinical practice
keyStudies.finding: Comprehensive hormone panels enable early detection of endocrine disorders and allow personalized treatment optimization with better outcomes than symptom-based approaches alone.

treatments.mechanism: Blood tests measure circulating hormone levels, binding proteins, and metabolic markers. Morning fasting draws are standard because hormones follow circadian rhythms. Comprehensive panels assess the entire endocrine axis to identify patterns of dysfunction.

treatments.name: Tai Chi
treatments.description: An ancient Chinese martial art practiced as a slow, flowing form of exercise. Harvard-endorsed as one of the best exercises for healthy aging. Strong evidence for balance, fall prevention, cardiovascular health, cognitive function, and stress reduction.

outcomes.name: Balance & Fall Prevention
outcomes.description: Reduces fall risk by 20-50% in older adults. Cochrane review confirms significant improvement in balance, gait, and proprioception.

outcomes.name: Cardiovascular Health
outcomes.description: Reduces blood pressure comparable to moderate aerobic exercise. Improves endothelial function and lipid profiles.

outcomes.name: Cognitive Function
outcomes.description: Improved executive function, processing speed, and memory in older adults. May delay cognitive decline.

quotes.text: Daily tai chi practice has transformed my balance and reduced my chronic pain significantly. At 65, I feel more stable than I did at 50

protocols.label: Beginner
protocols.notes: Start with simplified Yang style (24 forms). Focus on posture and breathing.

protocols.label: Daily Practice
protocols.notes: Full form practice. Combines with Qi Gong warm-ups. Morning practice preferred.

keyStudies.title: Tai Chi and fall prevention in older adults: a systematic review and meta-analysis
keyStudies.finding: Tai Chi reduces the rate of falls by 20-50% in older adults. Benefits are dose-dependent — more practice yields greater protection.

treatments.mechanism: Tai Chi integrates slow, controlled movements with deep breathing and mental focus. This simultaneously challenges proprioception, vestibular function, muscle coordination, and cardiovascular fitness. The meditative component reduces cortisol and activates the parasympathetic nervous system.
treatments.costEstimate: AED 200-500/month for classes; free for self-practice

treatments.name: Pilates
treatments.description: A low-impact exercise system focused on core strength, posture, flexibility, and body awareness. Created by Joseph Pilates in the early 20th century. Strong evidence for back pain, injury rehabilitation, and functional strength for aging well.

outcomes.name: Core Strength & Stability
outcomes.description: Significantly strengthens deep core muscles (transversus abdominis, multifidus). Improves functional stability for daily activities.

outcomes.name: Back Pain Reduction
outcomes.description: Systematic review: significantly reduces chronic low back pain. Comparable to other exercise-based interventions.

outcomes.name: Flexibility & Mobility
outcomes.description: Improves range of motion across all joints. Particularly beneficial for hip and shoulder flexibility.

quotes.text: Reformer Pilates 3x/week for 6 months completely resolved my chronic lower back pain. Core strength is transformative

protocols.label: Mat Pilates
protocols.notes: Bodyweight-based. Accessible anywhere. Focus on breathing and precision.

protocols.label: Reformer Pilates
protocols.notes: Machine-based with spring resistance. More challenging and targeted than mat work.

keyStudies.title: Pilates for low back pain: a systematic review
keyStudies.finding: Pilates significantly reduces chronic low back pain and disability compared to minimal intervention. Quality of evidence rated moderate-to-high.

treatments.mechanism: Pilates emphasizes controlled movements that target the deep stabilizing muscles of the core and spine. The focus on alignment, breathing, and proprioceptive awareness retrains movement patterns, reducing compensatory strain and improving overall biomechanical efficiency.

treatments.name: Circadian Rhythm Optimization
treatments.description: A structured approach to aligning daily light exposure, meal timing, exercise, and sleep with the body's master circadian clock. Foundational to Huberman's and Attia's protocols. Morning sunlight, timed eating, and blue-light management form the core pillars.

outcomes.name: Sleep Quality
outcomes.description: Morning light exposure within 30 min of waking sets the circadian clock, improving sleep onset, depth, and consistency. Effects well-established.

outcomes.name: Metabolic Health
outcomes.description: Time-restricted eating aligned with circadian rhythms improves insulin sensitivity, reduces inflammation, and optimizes metabolic function.

outcomes.name: Hormone Optimization
outcomes.description: Proper light timing optimizes cortisol awakening response, melatonin secretion, testosterone rhythms, and growth hormone release.

quotes.text: 10 minutes of morning sunlight + no screens after 9pm transformed my sleep within a week. This is the foundational protocol everyone should start with

protocols.label: Huberman Protocol
protocols.notes: Within 30 min of waking: 10-20 min outdoor sunlight (no sunglasses). Delay caffeine 90 min. Dim lights after sunset. No screens 1h before bed. Cool bedroom (18-19°C).

protocols.label: Time-Restricted Eating
protocols.notes: First meal 1-2h after waking, last meal 3h before sleep. Aligns feeding with circadian metabolic peak.

keyStudies.title: Circadian regulation of glucose, lipid, and energy metabolism in humans
keyStudies.finding: Comprehensive review: circadian misalignment disrupts glucose tolerance, lipid metabolism, and energy balance. Proper alignment reduces metabolic disease risk significantly.

treatments.mechanism: The suprachiasmatic nucleus (SCN) in the hypothalamus acts as the master circadian clock, synchronized primarily by light input to the retina. This clock coordinates peripheral clocks in every organ, timing hormone release, metabolic processes, DNA repair, and immune function across the 24-hour cycle.

treatments.name: Ketamine-Assisted Therapy
treatments.description: Medically supervised ketamine treatment for treatment-resistant depression, PTSD, anxiety, and chronic pain. The only widely available legal psychedelic-assisted therapy. Produces rapid antidepressant effects within hours to days, compared to weeks for traditional medications.

outcomes.name: Depression Relief
outcomes.description: Rapid antidepressant effect within 24-72 hours. 70% response rate in treatment-resistant depression. FDA-approved (Spravato/esketamine).

outcomes.name: Suicidal Ideation Reduction
outcomes.description: Uniquely rapid reduction in suicidal thoughts — within hours. Only approved treatment specifically for acute suicidal ideation.

outcomes.name: PTSD Symptom Reduction
outcomes.description: Significant PTSD symptom improvement when combined with psychotherapy. Effects may require maintenance sessions.

quotes.text: After years of failed antidepressants, IV ketamine gave me relief within 48 hours of my first session. Maintenance infusions every 4-6 weeks keep it stable

protocols.label: IV Infusion Series
protocols.dosage: 0.5mg/kg IV over 40 min, 6 sessions over 2-3 weeks
protocols.notes: Standard induction protocol. Must be administered in clinical setting with monitoring.

protocols.label: Spravato (Intranasal)
protocols.notes: FDA-approved esketamine. Must be administered in certified clinic with 2-hour observation.

keyStudies.title: A randomized trial of an N-methyl-D-aspartate antagonist in treatment-resistant major depression
keyStudies.finding: Single IV ketamine infusion produced rapid antidepressant effects within 2 hours, with 71% response rate at 24 hours. Effects lasted up to 1 week.

common: Dissociation
Nausea
Dizziness
Elevated blood pressure
serious: Addiction potential
Psychotic episodes (rare)

treatments.mechanism: Ketamine blocks NMDA glutamate receptors, triggering a rapid cascade of synaptic plasticity. This increases BDNF, activates mTOR, and stimulates synaptogenesis in the prefrontal cortex — essentially creating new neural connections that counteract depression-related brain atrophy.

treatments.name: VO2 Max Training
treatments.description: Structured high-intensity interval training designed to increase maximal aerobic capacity (VO2 max). Peter Attia calls VO2 max the single strongest predictor of all-cause mortality. A 1-MET increase in cardiorespiratory fitness reduces mortality risk by 13%.

outcomes.name: All-Cause Mortality Reduction
outcomes.description: The strongest predictor of longevity. Moving from bottom 25% to above-average VO2 max reduces mortality risk by 5x. More predictive than smoking, diabetes, or cardiovascular disease.

outcomes.name: VO2 Max Improvement
outcomes.description: 4x4 interval training consistently improves VO2 max by 10-15% within 8-12 weeks. Norwegian 4x4 protocol is the gold standard.

outcomes.name: Cardiovascular Health
outcomes.description: Improves cardiac output, stroke volume, mitochondrial density, and capillary perfusion. Reverses age-related cardiac decline.

quotes.text: Norwegian 4x4 protocol twice a week for 3 months. VO2 max went from 38 to 46. The data on mortality reduction is undeniable

protocols.label: Norwegian 4x4 (Attia Protocol)
protocols.dosage: 4x4 min at 85-95% max HR, 3 min recovery, 1-2x/week
protocols.notes: Warm up 10 min. 4 intervals of 4 minutes at near-maximal effort. Active recovery between intervals. Cool down 5 min.

protocols.label: Tabata / Short Intervals
protocols.notes: More intense, shorter protocol. Original Tabata study showed 14% VO2 max improvement in 6 weeks.

keyStudies.title: Cardiorespiratory fitness and mortality: a dose-response meta-analysis
keyStudies.finding: In 122,007 patients, each 1-MET increase in fitness reduced all-cause mortality by 13%. Elite fitness (top 2.3%) had 80% lower mortality than the least fit group.

treatments.mechanism: VO2 max training stresses the cardiovascular system at near-maximal capacity, driving adaptations: increased stroke volume, cardiac output, mitochondrial biogenesis in muscle, capillarization, and oxygen extraction efficiency. These adaptations are the biological basis for the mortality reduction.
treatments.costEstimate: Free (self-guided) or AED 200-500/month with a trainer

treatments.name: Mobility Training
treatments.description: Dedicated joint mobility, stability, and range-of-motion training. Peter Attia's 'stability' pillar — the foundation that enables all other exercise. Prevents injury, maintains functional independence, and is the most undertrained aspect of most fitness programs.

outcomes.name: Injury Prevention
outcomes.description: Mobility work reduces injury risk by 30-50% in athletic populations. Addresses the movement limitations that lead to compensatory strain and chronic pain.

outcomes.name: Functional Independence
outcomes.description: Maintains the ability to perform daily tasks: getting off the floor, reaching overhead, squatting, carrying groceries. Critical for aging well.

outcomes.name: Pain Reduction
outcomes.description: Addresses root causes of chronic joint and back pain through improved alignment and movement patterns.

quotes.text: 20 minutes of daily mobility work has done more for my chronic pain than years of massage and chiropractic combined

protocols.label: Daily Maintenance
protocols.notes: CARs (Controlled Articular Rotations) for every joint. Focus on spine, hips, shoulders, ankles.

protocols.label: Attia's Stability Framework
protocols.notes: DNS (Dynamic Neuromuscular Stabilization) based. Focuses on foot stability, hip control, scapular mechanics, and spinal stability.

keyStudies.title: Joint range of motion and injury risk: a systematic review
keyStudies.finding: Structured mobility training significantly reduces musculoskeletal injury risk. Benefits most pronounced when mobility work is integrated into warm-up and cooldown routines.

treatments.mechanism: Mobility training works at multiple levels: synovial fluid production for joint lubrication, fascial hydration and glide, neural patterning for motor control, and progressive tissue adaptation for range of motion. CARs (Controlled Articular Rotations) stimulate joint health signals at every range.
treatments.costEstimate: Free (self-guided) or AED 200-400/month with a coach

treatments.name: Massage Therapy
treatments.description: The foundational hands-on bodywork modality encompassing Swedish, deep tissue, sports, and therapeutic massage. One of the most widely used complementary therapies globally with strong evidence for pain relief, stress reduction, and recovery optimization.

outcomes.name: Pain Relief
outcomes.description: Meta-analyses confirm significant reduction in chronic pain, particularly low back pain, neck pain, and tension headaches.

outcomes.name: Stress & Cortisol Reduction
outcomes.description: Reduces cortisol by 30% on average while increasing serotonin and dopamine by 28-31%.

outcomes.name: Sleep Improvement
outcomes.description: Improves sleep quality through parasympathetic activation and pain reduction. Benefits both sleep onset and duration.

quotes.text: Weekly deep tissue massage is non-negotiable for my recovery. Noticeable difference in sleep quality and stress levels

protocols.label: Recovery Maintenance
protocols.notes: Deep tissue or sports massage. Focus on problem areas. Regular schedule for cumulative benefits.

protocols.label: Active Recovery
protocols.notes: Lighter pressure. Swedish or relaxation massage for stress management and general wellness.

keyStudies.title: A meta-analysis of massage therapy research
keyStudies.finding: Meta-analysis of 37 studies: massage therapy significantly reduces anxiety, depression, cortisol, and pain. Multiple treatments produce larger effects than single sessions.

treatments.mechanism: Manual pressure stimulates mechanoreceptors, triggering parasympathetic nervous system activation (reduced heart rate, blood pressure, cortisol). Local effects include increased blood flow, reduced muscle tension, improved lymphatic drainage, and release of myofascial adhesions.

treatments.name: Chiropractic Care
treatments.description: Spinal manipulation and musculoskeletal alignment therapy. One of the most widely used complementary therapies globally with strong evidence for low back pain and moderate evidence for neck pain and headaches.

outcomes.name: Low Back Pain Relief
outcomes.description: ACP guidelines recommend spinal manipulation as first-line treatment for acute low back pain. Comparable to NSAIDs for pain relief.

outcomes.name: Neck Pain & Headaches
outcomes.description: Moderate evidence for cervicogenic headaches and mechanical neck pain. Benefits often seen within 6-12 sessions.

quotes.text: Regular chiropractic adjustments have kept my back pain-free for 3 years after a herniated disc

protocols.label: Acute Pain
protocols.notes: Initial intensive phase for acute issues. Typically reduces to maintenance schedule.

protocols.label: Maintenance
protocols.notes: Preventive alignment checks. Combined with exercise and stretching.

keyStudies.title: Spinal manipulation for low back pain: an updated systematic review
keyStudies.finding: Spinal manipulation provides modest improvements in pain and function for chronic low back pain, comparable to other recommended therapies (exercise, standard medical care).

treatments.mechanism: Spinal manipulation applies a controlled force to specific vertebral joints, restoring range of motion, reducing muscle spasm, and modulating pain signals via the gate control theory. This triggers reflex relaxation of paraspinal muscles and may reduce local inflammation.

treatments.name: Time-Restricted Eating
treatments.description: Confining all daily food intake to a consistent 6-10 hour window, aligned with circadian rhythms. Distinct from general intermittent fasting by emphasizing the timing window rather than caloric restriction. Huberman, Attia, and Satchin Panda's research form the evidence base.

outcomes.name: Metabolic Health
outcomes.description: Improves insulin sensitivity, reduces fasting glucose, improves lipid profiles. Benefits independent of caloric restriction.

outcomes.name: Body Composition
outcomes.description: Modest fat loss (1-3% body weight) without intentional caloric restriction. Preserves lean mass better than continuous caloric restriction.

outcomes.name: Autophagy & Cellular Repair
outcomes.description: Extended overnight fasting periods activate autophagy — cellular cleanup and recycling. Requires ~14+ hours of fasting to fully engage.

quotes.text: 16:8 eating window aligned with my circadian rhythm. Energy more stable throughout the day and dropped 5kg in 3 months without counting calories

protocols.label: 16:8 Protocol
protocols.notes: Most popular and sustainable. Example: eat 10am-6pm. No calories outside window (water, black coffee, tea OK).

protocols.label: Circadian TRE
protocols.notes: Satchin Panda's research: eat within 10h starting 1-2h after waking. Earlier eating window (e.g., 8am-6pm) preferred over late.

keyStudies.title: Ten-Hour Time-Restricted Eating Reduces Weight, Blood Pressure, and Atherogenic Lipids in Patients with Metabolic Syndrome
keyStudies.finding: 10-hour TRE in metabolic syndrome patients: reduced body weight, blood pressure, atherogenic lipids, and HbA1c over 12 weeks without calorie counting.

treatments.mechanism: TRE works primarily through circadian alignment: restricting food to daytime hours when metabolic machinery is most active. During the fasting period, insulin drops, activating AMPK, autophagy, and fat oxidation. The consistent eating window also entrains peripheral clocks in the liver, gut, and pancreas.

treatments.name: Microneedling
treatments.description: Creates controlled micro-injuries in the skin using fine needles, triggering a wound-healing cascade that produces new collagen and elastin. Effective for acne scars, wrinkles, hyperpigmentation, and overall skin rejuvenation. Can be combined with PRP ('vampire facial').

outcomes.name: Collagen Production
outcomes.description: Increases dermal collagen types I and III by up to 400% after a series of treatments. Confirmed by histological studies.

outcomes.name: Acne Scar Improvement
outcomes.description: 50-75% improvement in acne scarring after 3-6 sessions. One of the most effective non-ablative treatments for scars.

outcomes.name: Wrinkle Reduction
outcomes.description: Significant improvement in fine lines and skin texture. Results build over 3-6 months as new collagen matures.

quotes.text: 4 microneedling sessions with PRP completely transformed my acne scars. Skin texture is dramatically smoother

protocols.label: Standard Series
protocols.notes: Professional depth (1.0-2.5mm). Apply numbing cream 30 min before. 3-5 days downtime.

protocols.label: PRP Combination (Vampire Facial)
protocols.notes: Microneedling followed by PRP application. Enhanced collagen stimulation. Premium pricing.

keyStudies.title: A quantitative and morphologic analysis of collagen induction therapy with microneedling
keyStudies.finding: Histological analysis showed significant increase in collagen and elastin deposition 6 months after microneedling, with up to 400% increase in collagen types I and III.

treatments.mechanism: Fine needles create controlled micro-channels in the dermis, initiating a three-phase wound healing response: inflammation (growth factor release), proliferation (new collagen/elastin synthesis), and remodeling (mature collagen fiber organization). This replaces damaged tissue with new, structured dermal matrix.

treatments.name: Alpha-Ketoglutarate (AKG)
treatments.description: Key metabolic intermediate in the Krebs cycle that reduces inflammation, supports mitochondrial function, and extended lifespan 12% in mice. Bryan Johnson includes calcium-AKG in his stack.

outcomes.name: Lifespan Extension
outcomes.description: Extended lifespan 12% and reduced biological age markers in mice. Human RCT (Rejuvant) showed 8-year reduction in biological age.

outcomes.name: Inflammation Reduction
outcomes.description: Reduces chronic low-grade inflammation through multiple metabolic pathways.

quotes.text: Ca-AKG has been a steady addition to my longevity stack. Energy and recovery both improved

protocols.label: Longevity
protocols.notes: Take in the morning with food. Ca-AKG is the most studied form.

keyStudies.title: Alpha-ketoglutarate extends lifespan in C57BL/6 mice
keyStudies.finding: Ca-AKG extended median lifespan by 12% in female mice, reduced frailty index, and improved mTOR pathway signaling.

treatments.mechanism: AKG is a central Krebs cycle metabolite that declines with age. Supplementation supports mitochondrial energy production, inhibits TOR signaling, promotes collagen synthesis, and serves as a nitrogen scavenger.

treatments.name: Apigenin
treatments.description: A natural flavonoid found in chamomile, celery, and parsley. Part of Andrew Huberman's nightly sleep stack. Promotes sleep quality through mild anxiolytic effects and CD38 inhibition (preserving NAD+ levels).

outcomes.name: Sleep Quality
outcomes.description: Mild sedative and anxiolytic effects through GABA modulation. Part of Huberman's recommended sleep stack alongside magnesium and L-theanine.

outcomes.name: NAD+ Preservation
outcomes.description: Inhibits CD38, an enzyme that degrades NAD+. May help preserve NAD+ levels synergistically with NMN/NR supplementation.

quotes.text: 50mg apigenin before bed as part of Huberman's sleep stack. Noticeably calmer at bedtime

protocols.label: Sleep (Huberman Stack)
protocols.notes: Take 30-60 min before sleep with magnesium threonate (145mg) and L-theanine (200mg).

keyStudies.title: Apigenin: a promising molecule for cancer prevention
keyStudies.finding: Review of apigenin's biological activities including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and anxiolytic effects through GABA receptor modulation.

treatments.mechanism: Apigenin binds to GABA-A receptors as a positive allosteric modulator, producing mild anxiolytic and sedative effects. It also inhibits CD38 (an NAD+-consuming enzyme), potentially preserving cellular NAD+ levels.

treatments.name: L-Theanine
treatments.description: An amino acid found primarily in green tea that promotes relaxation without sedation. Increases alpha brain waves, enhances focus when paired with caffeine, and improves sleep quality. Core component of Huberman's sleep protocol.

outcomes.name: Relaxation & Alpha Waves
outcomes.description: Increases alpha brain wave activity within 30-45 minutes, producing a calm, focused state without drowsiness.

outcomes.name: Focus (with Caffeine)
outcomes.description: L-theanine + caffeine is one of the most studied nootropic stacks. Improves attention, accuracy, and task-switching while reducing caffeine jitteriness.

outcomes.name: Sleep Quality
outcomes.description: Improves sleep quality in anxiety-prone individuals. Does not induce drowsiness but eases transition to sleep.

quotes.text: 200mg L-theanine with my morning coffee. Smooth, focused energy without the jitters. Game changer

protocols.label: Focus Stack
protocols.notes: 1:1 or 2:1 ratio with caffeine. Example: 200mg L-theanine + 100mg caffeine.

protocols.label: Sleep (Huberman)
protocols.notes: Part of sleep stack with magnesium threonate and apigenin. Take 30-60 min before bed.

keyStudies.title: The effects of L-theanine, caffeine and their combination on cognition and mood
keyStudies.finding: L-theanine + caffeine combination improved speed and accuracy of attention tasks and reduced susceptibility to distracting information.

treatments.mechanism: L-theanine crosses the blood-brain barrier and increases GABA, serotonin, and dopamine levels while promoting alpha wave production in the brain. It modulates glutamate release, producing anxiolytic effects without sedation.

treatments.name: Tongkat Ali
treatments.description: A Southeast Asian herb with strong clinical evidence for testosterone optimization, stress reduction, and body composition improvement. Andrew Huberman's primary recommendation for natural testosterone support.

outcomes.name: Testosterone Increase
outcomes.description: Multiple RCTs show 15-37% increase in total testosterone, particularly in stressed or hypogonadal men.

outcomes.name: Cortisol Reduction
outcomes.description: Reduces cortisol by 16% in stressed adults. The testosterone benefit may be partially mediated by cortisol reduction.

quotes.text: 400mg standardized tongkat ali daily. Noticeable improvement in energy, libido, and morning testosterone symptoms

protocols.label: Huberman Protocol
protocols.notes: Standardized extract (2% eurycomanone). Take in the morning. Cycle 8 weeks on, 2 weeks off.

keyStudies.title: Effect of Tongkat Ali on stress hormones and psychological mood state
keyStudies.journal: Journal of the International Society of Sports Nutrition
keyStudies.finding: 200mg/day for 4 weeks: 37% reduction in cortisol, 16% increase in testosterone. Significant improvement in tension, anger, and confusion scores.

treatments.mechanism: Eurycomanone and other quassinoids in tongkat ali reduce cortisol, increase free testosterone by lowering SHBG, and may directly stimulate Leydig cell testosterone production.

treatments.name: Rhodiola Rosea
treatments.description: An Arctic adaptogen with strong evidence for mental and physical fatigue reduction, stress resilience, and cognitive performance under pressure. Used by Viking warriors, Soviet athletes, and cosmonauts.

outcomes.name: Anti-Fatigue
outcomes.description: Significant reduction in mental and physical fatigue in multiple RCTs. Effects seen within 1 week of supplementation.

outcomes.name: Stress Resilience
outcomes.description: Improves stress tolerance and reduces burnout symptoms. Modulates cortisol and catecholamine response.

quotes.text: 200mg Rhodiola in the morning has been incredible for sustained energy and focus without the crash

protocols.label: Anti-Fatigue
protocols.notes: SHR-5 extract standardized to 3% rosavins, 1% salidroside. Take in the morning on empty stomach.

keyStudies.title: Rhodiola rosea in stress induced fatigue — a double blind cross-over study
keyStudies.finding: 170mg Rhodiola significantly improved mental fatigue, cognitive function, and general well-being during stressful exam periods in medical students.

treatments.mechanism: Rhodiola's rosavins and salidroside modulate cortisol, serotonin, dopamine, and norepinephrine. It enhances cellular energy by supporting mitochondrial ATP production and activates AMPK, the cell's energy sensor.

treatments.name: Collagen Peptides
treatments.description: Hydrolyzed collagen protein that supports skin elasticity, joint health, bone density, and gut lining integrity. Bryan Johnson takes 12.5g daily. The most abundant protein in the body, declining significantly after age 25.

outcomes.name: Skin Health
outcomes.description: Meta-analysis: 90 days of collagen supplementation significantly improved skin hydration, elasticity, and wrinkle depth.

outcomes.name: Joint Health
outcomes.description: Reduces joint pain in athletes and osteoarthritis patients. Supports cartilage regeneration.

quotes.text: 10g collagen peptides daily for 3 months. Skin noticeably plumper and knee pain during running has decreased significantly

protocols.label: Standard
protocols.notes: Mix in coffee, smoothie, or water. Type I/III for skin; Type II for joints. Take with Vitamin C for enhanced synthesis.

keyStudies.title: Oral collagen supplementation: a systematic review of dermatological applications
keyStudies.finding: Systematic review of 11 RCTs: oral collagen peptides significantly improved skin elasticity, hydration, and dermal collagen density.

treatments.mechanism: Hydrolyzed collagen peptides are absorbed as di- and tripeptides (especially prolyl-hydroxyproline) that stimulate fibroblasts to produce new collagen. They also signal chondrocytes in joints to increase cartilage matrix production.

treatments.name: Probiotics
treatments.description: Live beneficial bacteria (and their metabolic byproducts — postbiotics) that support gut microbiome health, immune function, mental health (gut-brain axis), and metabolic optimization. Strain-specific effects are critical — not all probiotics are the same.

outcomes.name: Gut Health & Digestion
outcomes.description: Strain-specific benefits for IBS, diarrhea, bloating, and gut barrier function. Lactobacillus and Bifidobacterium strains most studied.

outcomes.name: Immune Function
outcomes.description: Reduces duration and severity of upper respiratory infections. Modulates gut-associated lymphoid tissue (GALT).

outcomes.name: Mental Health (Psychobiotics)
outcomes.description: Specific strains (L. rhamnosus, B. longum) reduce anxiety and depression scores via the gut-brain axis.

quotes.text: Seed DS-01 has been the only probiotic that noticeably improved my digestion and bloating

protocols.label: General Gut Health
protocols.notes: Multi-strain formula. Take on empty stomach or with light meal. Refrigerated strains preferred.

protocols.label: Post-Antibiotic Recovery
protocols.notes: High-dose restoration after antibiotics. Saccharomyces boulardii particularly useful.

keyStudies.title: Probiotics for the prevention of antibiotic-associated diarrhea and Clostridium difficile
keyStudies.finding: Meta-analysis: probiotics reduce antibiotic-associated diarrhea risk by 42% and C. difficile infection by 60%.

treatments.mechanism: Probiotics colonize the gut, competing with pathogens, strengthening the mucosal barrier, producing short-chain fatty acids (butyrate), and modulating immune responses via gut-associated lymphoid tissue. Postbiotics are the beneficial metabolites produced by these bacteria.

treatments.name: HRV Biofeedback
treatments.description: Real-time heart rate variability coherence training using sensors and software to improve stress resilience, autonomic balance, and emotional regulation. Proven to reduce anxiety, improve athletic performance, and optimize parasympathetic tone.

outcomes.name: Stress Reduction
outcomes.description: Significant reduction in perceived stress, anxiety, and depression scores through coherence training.

outcomes.name: Autonomic Balance
outcomes.description: Increases parasympathetic tone (vagal activity) and improves overall HRV — a key biomarker of health and resilience.

quotes.text: HeartMath coherence training 10 min/day has measurably improved my resting HRV from 45 to 62 over 3 months

protocols.label: Daily Practice
protocols.notes: Use HeartMath Inner Balance sensor or Elite HRV. Focus on slow, paced breathing at 6 breaths/minute.

keyStudies.title: Heart rate variability biofeedback: how and why does it work?
keyStudies.finding: HRV biofeedback at resonance frequency (0.1 Hz) strengthens baroreflex, improves autonomic function, and reduces symptoms of stress, anxiety, depression, and PTSD.

treatments.mechanism: HRV biofeedback trains the baroreflex arc by having users breathe at their resonance frequency (~0.1 Hz / 6 breaths per minute). This creates maximal oscillations in heart rate, strengthening the baroreceptor reflex and improving autonomic nervous system regulation.
treatments.costEstimate: AED 200-500 for device; free ongoing practice

treatments.name: LED Light Therapy (Skin)
treatments.description: Different wavelengths of LED light target specific skin concerns: red light (630-660nm) for collagen and wrinkles, blue light (415nm) for acne bacteria, NIR (830nm) for deep tissue healing. Available as professional treatments and at-home devices.

outcomes.name: Collagen Stimulation
outcomes.description: Red LED at 630-660nm increases fibroblast activity and collagen synthesis. Studies show improved skin texture and reduced wrinkle depth after 12 weeks.

outcomes.name: Acne Reduction
outcomes.description: Blue LED at 415nm kills P. acnes bacteria. Combined blue-red protocols reduce inflammatory acne by 75% in clinical trials.

quotes.text: Omnilux Contour Face mask 10 min daily. Skin glow is noticeable after 6 weeks and my acne scars are fading

protocols.label: At-Home Device
protocols.notes: LED face mask or panel. Red for anti-aging, blue for acne. Consistent daily use required.

protocols.label: Professional Treatment
protocols.notes: Higher intensity devices. Often combined with facials or post-procedure healing.

keyStudies.title: A controlled trial to determine the efficacy of red and near-infrared light treatment in patient satisfaction, reduction of fine lines, wrinkles, skin roughness, and intradermal collagen density increase
keyStudies.finding: Red and NIR LED treatment significantly improved skin complexion, texture, and collagen density with high subject satisfaction.

treatments.mechanism: LED photons are absorbed by mitochondrial chromophores (cytochrome c oxidase), enhancing ATP production and triggering cell signaling cascades. Red light stimulates fibroblast collagen synthesis; blue light generates reactive oxygen species that kill P. acnes bacteria.
treatments.costEstimate: AED 100-300/session or AED 1,000-3,000 for home device

treatments.name: Chemical Peels
treatments.description: Controlled chemical exfoliation using acids (glycolic, salicylic, TCA, phenol) to remove damaged skin layers and stimulate renewal. Treats hyperpigmentation, acne scars, fine lines, and uneven texture.

outcomes.name: Hyperpigmentation Improvement
outcomes.description: Glycolic and TCA peels significantly improve melasma, sun spots, and post-inflammatory hyperpigmentation.

outcomes.name: Skin Texture & Fine Lines
outcomes.description: Progressive improvement in skin texture and fine lines with series of medium-depth peels.

quotes.text: Series of 4 glycolic peels cleared my melasma significantly. Skin is more even and radiant

protocols.label: Light Peel Series
protocols.notes: Glycolic 30-50% or salicylic 20-30%. Minimal downtime (1-3 days). Good maintenance protocol.

protocols.label: Medium Depth
protocols.notes: TCA 15-35%. More significant peeling (5-7 days downtime). Dramatic results for scarring and sun damage.

keyStudies.title: Chemical peeling in the treatment of acne
keyStudies.finding: Serial glycolic acid peels significantly improved acne severity scores and post-inflammatory hyperpigmentation.

treatments.mechanism: Chemical agents break intercellular bonds in the epidermis, causing controlled desquamation. This triggers a wound-healing response: inflammation → fibroblast activation → new collagen/elastin deposition → smoother, more even skin.

treatments.name: RF Microneedling
treatments.description: Combines microneedling with radiofrequency energy delivery at adjustable depths. Morpheus8 is the most popular device. More effective than standard microneedling for skin tightening, body contouring, and deep scar remodeling.

outcomes.name: Skin Tightening
outcomes.description: RF energy delivered at depth causes tissue contraction and collagen remodeling. Superior to standard microneedling for laxity.

outcomes.name: Deep Scar Remodeling
outcomes.description: Adjustable depth allows treatment of deep acne scars and stretch marks. Better results than surface-level treatments.

quotes.text: Morpheus8 on face and neck — dramatic tightening after 2 sessions. Jawline looks noticeably more defined

protocols.label: Face & Neck
protocols.notes: Numbing cream required. Depth 1-4mm adjustable. 3-5 days downtime.

protocols.label: Body (Stretch Marks/Laxity)
protocols.notes: Deeper needle penetration for body areas. Abdomen, thighs, arms.

keyStudies.title: Fractional radiofrequency microneedling: a systematic review
keyStudies.finding: Systematic review confirms RF microneedling significantly improves acne scars, skin laxity, and wrinkles with favorable safety profile and minimal downtime.

treatments.mechanism: Insulated microneedles penetrate to a set depth, then deliver bipolar RF energy directly into the dermis. The combined mechanical and thermal injury triggers an enhanced wound-healing response with greater collagen/elastin remodeling than either modality alone.

treatments.name: Functional Fitness
treatments.description: Training focused on real-world movement patterns — squatting, lifting, carrying, pushing, pulling, and rotational movements. Builds practical strength that maintains independence and quality of life as you age.

outcomes.name: Daily Function Preservation
outcomes.description: Maintains ability to perform activities of daily living: getting off the floor, carrying groceries, climbing stairs. Critical for aging independently.

outcomes.name: Injury Prevention
outcomes.description: Multi-plane movement training reduces injury risk by improving stabilizer muscle strength and movement competency.

quotes.text: Functional training — farmer carries, Turkish get-ups, loaded carries — has made me genuinely stronger in real life, not just in the gym

protocols.label: Foundation
protocols.notes: Focus on compound movements: squat, deadlift, press, pull, carry, hip hinge. Progressive overload.

protocols.label: Longevity-Focused
protocols.notes: Attia's centenarian decathlon concept: train for activities you want to do at 90.

keyStudies.title: Functional training vs traditional resistance training on physical performance in older adults
keyStudies.journal: Journal of Aging and Physical Activity
keyStudies.finding: Functional training improved balance, gait speed, and stair-climbing ability more than traditional machine-based resistance training in older adults.

treatments.mechanism: Functional movements recruit multiple muscle groups across multiple planes simultaneously, training the neuromuscular system to coordinate complex movement patterns. This builds both strength and motor control — the combination needed for real-world physical competency.
treatments.costEstimate: AED 200-500/month for gym; free for bodyweight

treatments.name: Stretching & Flexibility
treatments.description: Dedicated flexibility practice including static stretching, dynamic stretching, and PNF techniques. Fundamental recovery and injury prevention modality that maintains range of motion as connective tissue naturally stiffens with age.

outcomes.name: Range of Motion
outcomes.description: Consistent stretching increases joint ROM by 10-30% over 4-8 weeks. Static stretching held 30-60 seconds is most effective.

outcomes.name: Injury Prevention
outcomes.description: Dynamic stretching in warm-ups reduces muscle strain risk. Static stretching as cooldown reduces DOMS.

quotes.text: 15 minutes of daily stretching has eliminated the chronic tightness I'd had for years. Simple but transformative

protocols.label: Daily Maintenance
protocols.notes: Major muscle groups: hamstrings, hip flexors, chest, lats, calves. Hold static stretches 30-60 seconds.

protocols.label: Warm-Up Dynamic
protocols.notes: Leg swings, arm circles, walking lunges, high knees. Dynamic movements that prepare joints for full ROM.

keyStudies.title: Impact of stretching on muscle soreness and injury risk: a systematic review
keyStudies.finding: Stretching reduces injury risk modestly. Static stretching improves flexibility reliably. Dynamic stretching before exercise maintains performance while reducing strain risk.

treatments.mechanism: Stretching applies tension to the muscle-tendon unit, causing viscoelastic deformation. Over time, this increases sarcomere number (muscle length) and reduces passive tension in connective tissue. Neural adaptation also reduces stretch reflex sensitivity, allowing greater ROM.

treatments.name: Qi Gong
treatments.description: An ancient Chinese practice combining gentle movements, breathing, and meditation to cultivate and balance 'qi' (life energy). Strong evidence for balance, cognitive function, chronic pain, and quality of life in older adults.

outcomes.name: Balance & Fall Prevention
outcomes.description: Significantly improves balance and reduces fall risk in older adults. Benefits similar to Tai Chi.

outcomes.name: Chronic Pain Management
outcomes.description: Reduces chronic pain symptoms across multiple conditions including fibromyalgia, arthritis, and low back pain.

quotes.text: Daily qi gong practice has dramatically improved my energy levels and chronic back pain. The gentleness is deceptive — it's incredibly effective

protocols.label: Daily Practice
protocols.notes: Eight Brocades (Ba Duan Jin) is the most studied and accessible form. Morning practice preferred.

keyStudies.title: Health benefits of Qi Gong and Tai Chi: a systematic review
keyStudies.finding: Comprehensive review of 77 studies: Qi Gong and Tai Chi improve bone density, cardiopulmonary function, physical function, falls prevention, quality of life, and psychological symptoms.

treatments.mechanism: Qi Gong combines slow, intentional movement with diaphragmatic breathing and focused attention. This activates the parasympathetic nervous system, improves proprioceptive function, stimulates blood and lymph circulation, and promotes neuroplasticity through mindful movement practice.
treatments.costEstimate: AED 100-300/month for classes; free for self-practice

treatments.name: Dry Needling
treatments.description: Uses acupuncture-style needles inserted directly into myofascial trigger points to release muscle tension and pain. Distinct from acupuncture — based on Western neuroanatomy rather than meridian theory. Popular in sports medicine and physical therapy.

outcomes.name: Trigger Point Release
outcomes.description: Effective at deactivating myofascial trigger points, reducing referred pain patterns, and restoring range of motion.

outcomes.name: Chronic Pain Reduction
outcomes.description: Reduces chronic neck, shoulder, and back pain. Meta-analysis shows short-term benefits comparable to other manual therapies.

quotes.text: Dry needling released tension in my upper trapezius that no amount of massage could touch. Immediate relief

protocols.label: Acute Treatment
protocols.notes: Needle inserted into trigger point, producing a 'twitch response'. Soreness normal for 24-48 hours.

protocols.label: Maintenance
protocols.notes: Preventive trigger point management. Often combined with exercise and stretching.

keyStudies.title: Dry needling for myofascial trigger points: a systematic review and meta-analysis
keyStudies.journal: Archives of Physical Medicine and Rehabilitation
keyStudies.finding: Dry needling provides significant short-term pain reduction for myofascial trigger point pain, with effects comparable to or slightly better than sham needling.

treatments.mechanism: The needle mechanically disrupts the trigger point's contraction knot, eliciting a local twitch response that resets the dysfunctional motor endplate. This reduces acetylcholine release, normalizes pH in the trigger point, and breaks the pain-spasm-ischemia cycle.

treatments.name: Craniosacral Therapy
treatments.description: A gentle hands-on technique that uses light touch to evaluate and release restrictions in the craniosacral system — the membranes and fluid surrounding the brain and spinal cord. Used for headaches, chronic pain, and nervous system regulation.

outcomes.name: Headache & Migraine Relief
outcomes.description: Several studies show reduced headache frequency and intensity. Particularly effective for tension-type headaches and migraines.

outcomes.name: Nervous System Regulation
outcomes.description: Promotes parasympathetic activation and deep relaxation. Benefits for chronic stress, sleep, and trauma resolution.

quotes.text: Craniosacral therapy put me into the deepest state of relaxation I've ever experienced. My chronic headaches have reduced significantly after 4 sessions

protocols.label: Series
protocols.notes: 60-minute sessions. Extremely gentle touch (5g pressure). Fully clothed. Deep relaxation common.

keyStudies.title: Craniosacral therapy for chronic pain: a systematic review
keyStudies.finding: Preliminary evidence supports CST for chronic neck pain, migraine, and fibromyalgia. More high-quality RCTs needed.

treatments.mechanism: CST practitioners detect and release restrictions in the craniosacral system through light touch on the skull, sacrum, and spine. This normalizes the flow of cerebrospinal fluid, releases fascial restrictions, and promotes parasympathetic nervous system activation.

treatments.name: Heavy Metal Testing
treatments.description: Testing for accumulated levels of toxic heavy metals — lead, mercury, arsenic, cadmium, and aluminum. Foundational to functional medicine detox protocols. Can be performed via blood, urine, or provoked urine challenge test.

outcomes.name: Toxic Burden Assessment
outcomes.description: Identifies elevated levels of toxic metals that contribute to fatigue, cognitive issues, and chronic disease risk.

outcomes.name: Treatment Guidance
outcomes.description: Results guide targeted chelation therapy or dietary detox protocols.

quotes.text: Heavy metal panel revealed elevated mercury levels from years of sushi consumption. Targeted detox protocol made a noticeable difference in brain fog

protocols.label: Baseline Panel
protocols.notes: Tests: lead, mercury, arsenic, cadmium, aluminum. Fasting not required for most tests.

protocols.label: Provoked Challenge Test
protocols.notes: More sensitive test using DMSA or EDTA to mobilize stored metals. Reveals tissue burden.

keyStudies.title: Heavy metals toxicity and the environment
keyStudies.finding: Heavy metal accumulation contributes to neurological damage, kidney disease, cardiovascular disease, and cancer. Regular testing enables early intervention.

treatments.mechanism: Blood tests measure acute/recent exposure; urine tests measure excretion. Provoked challenge tests use chelating agents to mobilize metals from tissue stores, providing a more accurate picture of total body burden.

treatments.name: Food Sensitivity Testing
treatments.description: IgG-mediated food sensitivity testing to identify delayed immune reactions to specific foods. Used in functional medicine to guide elimination diets, reduce chronic inflammation, and heal gut permeability.

outcomes.name: Inflammation Identification
outcomes.description: Identifies foods triggering IgG immune responses. Elimination of reactive foods often reduces chronic inflammation, bloating, and skin issues. Evidence debated.

outcomes.name: Elimination Diet Guidance
outcomes.description: Provides a personalized starting point for elimination diets, which are the gold standard for identifying food triggers.

community.timeToEffect: Results in 1-2 weeks; benefits 2-6 weeks after elimination

quotes.text: Food sensitivity test identified dairy and eggs as triggers. Eliminating them resolved chronic bloating and skin breakouts within 3 weeks

protocols.label: Standard Panel
protocols.notes: Tests IgG antibody levels against common foods. Results guide a 30-60 day elimination protocol.

protocols.label: Elimination + Reintroduction
protocols.notes: Eliminate all reactive foods for 30 days, then reintroduce one at a time every 3-4 days to confirm reactions.

keyStudies.title: Food-specific IgG4 antibody-guided exclusion diet improves symptoms and rectal compliance in IBS
keyStudies.finding: IgG4-guided elimination diet significantly improved IBS symptoms and rectal compliance compared to sham diet.

treatments.mechanism: IgG food sensitivity tests measure immunoglobulin G antibodies against food proteins. Elevated IgG may indicate increased gut permeability (leaky gut) allowing food proteins to enter the bloodstream, triggering delayed immune reactions and chronic low-grade inflammation.

treatments.name: Ketogenic Diet
treatments.description: A very low-carbohydrate, high-fat diet that shifts metabolism to produce ketone bodies for fuel. Originally developed for epilepsy, now researched for brain health, metabolic syndrome, cancer adjunct therapy, and weight management.

outcomes.name: Weight & Fat Loss
outcomes.description: Effective for rapid weight loss in the short-to-medium term. Meta-analyses show greater weight loss than low-fat diets at 6 months; difference narrows at 12 months.

outcomes.name: Epilepsy Control
outcomes.description: The original medical application. 50%+ seizure reduction in ~50% of drug-resistant epilepsy patients.

outcomes.name: Brain Health & Neuroprotection
outcomes.description: Ketones provide an alternative brain fuel. Emerging evidence for Alzheimer's, Parkinson's, and traumatic brain injury.

community.timeToEffect: 1-2 weeks for ketosis; 4-8 weeks for full adaptation

quotes.text: Strict keto for 3 months cleared my brain fog completely and I lost 12kg. Now I cycle between keto and Mediterranean

protocols.label: Standard Keto
protocols.notes: Strict carb restriction. Focus on healthy fats (olive oil, avocado, nuts). Monitor ketones via blood meter.

protocols.label: Cyclical Keto
protocols.notes: More sustainable long-term. Keto weekdays, moderate carbs on weekends. Supports exercise performance.

keyStudies.title: Dietary ketosis enhances memory in mild cognitive impairment
keyStudies.finding: 6-week very low-carb diet improved verbal memory performance in older adults with mild cognitive impairment, correlated with ketone levels.

treatments.mechanism: Severe carbohydrate restriction depletes liver glycogen, forcing the liver to convert fatty acids into ketone bodies (BHB, AcAc, acetone). Ketones serve as an efficient alternative fuel for the brain and muscles, while reducing insulin levels and activating AMPK/autophagy pathways.

treatments.name: Caloric Restriction
treatments.description: The most researched longevity intervention in history — a sustained 10-30% reduction in caloric intake without malnutrition. Extends lifespan in nearly every organism tested, from yeast to primates. The CALERIE human trial confirmed metabolic and aging benefits.

outcomes.name: Biological Aging Slowdown
outcomes.description: CALERIE trial: 25% CR for 2 years slowed biological aging by 2-3% per year measured by DunedinPACE epigenetic clock.

outcomes.name: Metabolic Health
outcomes.description: Reduces fasting glucose, insulin, blood pressure, cholesterol, inflammation (CRP), and oxidative stress markers.

outcomes.name: Lifespan Extension (Animal)
outcomes.description: Extends lifespan 20-50% in rodents, 10-20% in primates. The most consistently replicated longevity intervention across species.

quotes.text: 15% caloric restriction for 2 years. Blood markers are remarkable but adherence is the hardest part of any longevity protocol

protocols.label: Moderate CR
protocols.notes: Most sustainable. Focus on nutrient density. Avoid malnutrition — protein intake must remain adequate (1.2-1.6g/kg).

protocols.label: CALERIE Protocol
protocols.notes: Based on the CALERIE clinical trial. Requires careful nutritional planning. Medical supervision recommended.

keyStudies.title: Caloric restriction slows biological aging in the CALERIE trial
keyStudies.finding: 2-year 25% caloric restriction in non-obese adults slowed the pace of biological aging by 2-3% per year as measured by the DunedinPACE epigenetic clock.

treatments.mechanism: CR activates multiple longevity pathways simultaneously: AMPK activation, mTOR inhibition, sirtuin activation, autophagy enhancement, reduced insulin/IGF-1 signaling, decreased inflammation, and improved mitochondrial efficiency. These converge to slow the pace of biological aging.

treatments.name: Pelvic Floor Therapy
treatments.description: Specialized physical therapy for pelvic floor dysfunction affecting both men and women. Addresses urinary incontinence, pelvic pain, sexual dysfunction, and post-surgical recovery. One of the most undertreated conditions in healthcare.

outcomes.name: Urinary Incontinence
outcomes.description: First-line treatment for stress and urge incontinence. 70-80% improvement in symptoms with structured pelvic floor rehabilitation.

outcomes.name: Sexual Function
outcomes.description: Improves erectile function in men and sexual satisfaction in women through pelvic floor strengthening and relaxation.

outcomes.name: Chronic Pelvic Pain
outcomes.description: Reduces chronic pelvic pain through trigger point release, myofascial therapy, and neuromuscular re-education.

quotes.text: Pelvic floor therapy after prostate surgery restored continence within 6 weeks. Wish I'd known about it sooner

protocols.label: Standard Program
protocols.notes: Includes assessment, manual therapy, biofeedback, and progressive exercise program. Home exercises daily.

protocols.label: Kegel Training
protocols.notes: Proper technique is critical — many people do Kegels incorrectly. Professional assessment recommended first.

keyStudies.title: Pelvic floor muscle training for urinary incontinence in women
keyStudies.finding: Pelvic floor muscle training is effective for women with stress, urge, or mixed urinary incontinence. Women are 8x more likely to report cure with PFMT than controls.

treatments.mechanism: Pelvic floor therapy addresses dysfunction through strengthening (for weakness/incontinence), relaxation (for hypertonic/pain conditions), and coordination training. Biofeedback helps patients learn to isolate and control pelvic floor muscles they can't consciously feel.

treatments.name: Acoustic Wave Therapy
treatments.description: Non-invasive sound wave therapy that improves blood flow and stimulates tissue regeneration. Primarily used for erectile dysfunction, Peyronie's disease, and cellulite reduction with growing clinical evidence.

outcomes.name: Improved erectile function
outcomes.description: Improved erectile function

outcomes.name: Enhanced blood flow
outcomes.description: Enhanced blood flow

outcomes.name: Tissue regeneration
outcomes.description: Tissue regeneration

outcomes.name: Pain reduction
outcomes.description: Pain reduction

quotes.text: Noticeable improvement in 6-12 sessions
Non-invasive alternative to medication
Expensive per session

protocols.label: Standard ED Protocol
protocols.dosage: 6-12 sessions over 6-12 weeks, 15-20 minutes per session using low-intensity focused shockwaves

keyStudies.title: Li-ESWT for Erectile Dysfunction: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis
keyStudies.finding: Significant improvement in erectile function scores vs placebo across 7 RCTs

treatments.name: PRP Shot (P-Shot / O-Shot)
treatments.fullName: Platelet-Rich Plasma Injection for Sexual Health
treatments.description: Platelet-rich plasma injections for sexual rejuvenation. The P-Shot targets male erectile tissue while the O-Shot targets female sexual response areas, using the body's own growth factors to stimulate regeneration.

outcomes.name: Enhanced sexual response
outcomes.description: Enhanced sexual response

outcomes.name: Improved sensitivity
outcomes.description: Improved sensitivity

outcomes.name: Tissue rejuvenation
outcomes.description: Tissue rejuvenation

quotes.text: Noticeable improvement in sensitivity
Uses own blood — no foreign substances
Expensive

protocols.label: Standard PRP Shot
protocols.dosage: Single injection using concentrated platelet-rich plasma from patient's own blood, repeated every 12-18 months

keyStudies.title: PRP for Erectile Dysfunction: Pilot Study
keyStudies.finding: Preliminary evidence of improved erectile function scores in small pilot study

treatments.name: Fertility Optimization
treatments.description: Evidence-based protocols combining supplementation, lifestyle modifications, and targeted interventions to optimize reproductive health. Includes CoQ10, folate, zinc, antioxidants, and hormonal balancing strategies.

outcomes.name: Improved sperm quality
outcomes.description: Improved sperm quality

outcomes.name: Enhanced egg health
outcomes.description: Enhanced egg health

outcomes.name: Hormonal balance
outcomes.description: Hormonal balance

outcomes.name: Higher conception rates
outcomes.description: Higher conception rates

quotes.text: Comprehensive approach addresses multiple factors
Many supplements are well-tolerated
Takes 3-6 months to see results

protocols.label: Male Fertility Protocol
protocols.dosage: CoQ10 200-600mg, zinc 30mg, folate, vitamin D, omega-3, antioxidants for 3-6 months

protocols.label: Female Fertility Protocol
protocols.dosage: CoQ10, folate/methylfolate, vitamin D, DHEA (if indicated), inositol for 3-6 months before conception

keyStudies.title: Antioxidant Supplementation and Male Fertility
keyStudies.finding: Antioxidant supplementation improved sperm concentration, motility, and morphology in subfertile men

treatments.name: Hormone Pellet Therapy
treatments.description: Subcutaneous pellet implants that deliver consistent bioidentical hormones (testosterone, estradiol) over 3-6 months. Provides steady-state hormone levels without daily application or weekly injections.

outcomes.name: Stable hormone levels
outcomes.description: Stable hormone levels

outcomes.name: Improved libido
outcomes.description: Improved libido

outcomes.name: Better energy and mood
outcomes.description: Better energy and mood

outcomes.name: Reduced menopausal symptoms
outcomes.description: Reduced menopausal symptoms

quotes.text: Consistent hormone delivery
No daily maintenance
Pellet extrusion possible

protocols.label: Standard Pellet Insertion
protocols.dosage: Small pellets inserted subcutaneously in the hip area every 3-6 months under local anesthesia

keyStudies.title: Subcutaneous Testosterone Pellet Therapy in Women
keyStudies.finding: Effective for improving sexual desire and satisfaction with favorable safety profile

treatments.name: Vaginal Rejuvenation Laser
treatments.fullName: Fractional CO2 Laser Vaginal Rejuvenation (MonaLisa Touch)
treatments.description: Fractional CO2 laser therapy for vaginal atrophy, dryness, and urinary incontinence. Stimulates collagen production and tissue remodeling in vaginal mucosa, primarily for postmenopausal women.

outcomes.name: Reduced vaginal dryness
outcomes.description: Reduced vaginal dryness

outcomes.name: Improved urinary continence
outcomes.description: Improved urinary continence

outcomes.name: Enhanced tissue elasticity

outcomes.name: Better sexual comfort
outcomes.description: Better sexual comfort

quotes.text: Significant relief from dryness
Quick outpatient procedure
Temporary discomfort

protocols.label: Standard Protocol
protocols.dosage: 3 sessions spaced 6 weeks apart, with annual maintenance treatment

keyStudies.title: Fractional CO2 Laser for Genitourinary Syndrome of Menopause
keyStudies.finding: Significant improvement in vaginal dryness, dyspareunia, and urinary symptoms in postmenopausal women

treatments.name: Men's Health Optimization
treatments.description: Integrated approach to male health combining hormone optimization, cardiovascular fitness, pelvic floor training, and targeted supplementation. Addresses libido, performance, prostate health, and longevity.

outcomes.name: Optimized testosterone

Addresses root causes not just symptoms

protocols.label: Core Protocol
protocols.dosage: Comprehensive hormone panel, targeted supplementation (zinc, boron, vitamin D, tongkat ali), cardiovascular exercise, pelvic floor work, and sleep optimization

keyStudies.title: Lifestyle Modifications and Testosterone Levels
keyStudies.finding: Exercise, sleep optimization, and stress reduction significantly improved testosterone levels in middle-aged men

treatments.name: Mediterranean Diet
treatments.description: Plant-rich dietary pattern emphasizing olive oil, fish, whole grains, legumes, vegetables, and moderate wine. One of the most studied diets with strong evidence for cardiovascular health, longevity, and cognitive protection.

outcomes.name: Reduced cardiovascular risk
outcomes.description: Reduced cardiovascular risk

outcomes.name: Lower all-cause mortality
outcomes.description: Lower all-cause mortality

outcomes.name: Cognitive protection
outcomes.description: Cognitive protection

outcomes.name: Anti-inflammatory effects
outcomes.description: Anti-inflammatory effects

outcomes.name: Weight management
outcomes.description: Weight management

quotes.text: Sustainable and enjoyable
Flexible — not restrictive
Requires cooking skills

protocols.label: Standard Mediterranean
protocols.dosage: Daily: olive oil, vegetables, fruits, whole grains, legumes. Weekly: fish 2-3x, poultry 2x, eggs. Moderate red wine optional. Limited red meat, sweets.

keyStudies.title: PREDIMED Trial
keyStudies.finding: Mediterranean diet supplemented with extra-virgin olive oil reduced cardiovascular events by ~30% vs control diet

keyStudies.title: Mediterranean Diet and Mortality Meta-Analysis
keyStudies.finding: Adherence associated with 21% reduction in all-cause mortality across 29 studies

treatments.name: Carnivore Diet
treatments.description: All-animal-product diet eliminating plant foods entirely. Used as an elimination protocol for autoimmune conditions, gut issues, and inflammation. Controversial but growing community with reported benefits for specific conditions.

outcomes.name: Reduced inflammation markers

protocols.label: Standard Carnivore
protocols.dosage: Beef, salt, water as base. Gradually add eggs, butter, organ meats. Some include dairy. Minimum 30-90 day trial.

keyStudies.title: Harvard Carnivore Diet Survey
keyStudies.finding: Self-reported survey of 2,029 carnivore dieters showed high satisfaction, improved BMI, and reduced medication use

treatments.name: Fasting Mimicking Diet (FMD)
treatments.description: A 5-day low-calorie, plant-based meal plan designed to trigger fasting-like cellular responses while still allowing food intake. Developed by Valter Longo at USC Longevity Institute. Promotes autophagy, stem cell regeneration, and metabolic reset.

Structured meal plan removes guesswork

protocols.label: Standard FMD Cycle
protocols.dosage: 5-day protocol: Day 1 ~1,100 cal, Days 2-5 ~750 cal. Plant-based, high-fat, low-protein. Repeat every 1-3 months.

keyStudies.title: FMD and Markers of Aging
keyStudies.finding: 3 monthly FMD cycles reduced body weight, blood pressure, IGF-1, and biomarkers of aging in a randomized trial

keyStudies.title: Fasting-Mimicking Diet and Multi-System Regeneration
keyStudies.finding: FMD promoted multi-system regeneration, enhanced cognitive performance, and reduced cancer incidence in mice

treatments.name: Elimination Diet
treatments.description: Structured dietary protocol that removes common trigger foods (gluten, dairy, soy, eggs, corn, nuts, nightshades) for 3-6 weeks, then systematically reintroduces them to identify individual sensitivities and intolerances.

Identifies hidden triggers
Socially restrictive during elimination phase

protocols.label: Standard Elimination
protocols.dosage: Remove top allergens (gluten, dairy, soy, eggs, corn, nuts, nightshades, sugar) for 3-6 weeks. Reintroduce one food every 3 days, tracking symptoms.

keyStudies.title: Elimination Diet for IBS
keyStudies.finding: Elimination diet significantly reduced IBS symptoms compared to standard dietary advice

treatments.name: Anti-Inflammatory Diet
treatments.description: Dietary pattern focused on reducing systemic inflammation through omega-3-rich foods, colorful vegetables, berries, turmeric, green tea, and elimination of processed foods, refined sugars, and seed oils. Based on research linking chronic inflammation to aging and disease.

protocols.label: Core Anti-Inflammatory
protocols.dosage: Emphasize: fatty fish, leafy greens, berries, turmeric, olive oil, nuts. Eliminate: refined sugar, processed foods, trans fats, excess omega-6 oils. Daily green tea or matcha.

keyStudies.title: Dietary Inflammatory Index and Mortality
keyStudies.finding: Higher dietary inflammatory index associated with increased all-cause mortality across multiple cohort studies

treatments.costEstimate: Normal to moderately increased grocery budget

treatments.name: Low FODMAP Diet
treatments.fullName: Low Fermentable Oligosaccharides, Disaccharides, Monosaccharides and Polyols Diet
treatments.description: Clinically validated dietary approach for managing IBS and SIBO symptoms. Restricts specific fermentable carbohydrates that cause gas, bloating, and digestive distress, followed by systematic reintroduction to identify triggers.
